contexts.context: ...vindicate this incident, the well known story of <mark>Kirk</mark> may be told—but that proves no more than...
...Inchbald's note.] Inchbald alludes to Colonel Percy <mark>Kirke</mark>, an officer in the service of James II...
...tyrant during Monmouth's Rebellion. David Hume recounts <mark>Kirke's</mark> story in his The History of England, from...
...VIII, chapter LXXI. According to Hume, <mark>Kirke</mark> sexually extorted a young woman during one of his...
...exchange for her compliance. The next morning, <mark>Kirke</mark> led the woman to the window of the room...
...her brother's corpse, hanging on a gibbet which <mark>Kirke</mark> had specially ordered to be constructed outside of his...
..., to a Rose, Brought from Near Alloway <mark>Kirk</mark>, in Ayrshire, in the Autumn of 1822...
